title: NSCLC Group-Sequential SAP
summary: Produce a statistical analysis plan package for a Phase III NSCLC trial with log-rank sample size, O'Brien-Fleming interim boundaries, futility, Hochberg secondary testing, and plots.
taskPrompt:
You are preparing a clinical-trial Statistical Analysis Plan on a Linux VM.

## Task Folder
`/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/healthcare_sap_group_sequential_nsclc/base`

## Inputs
- Protocol JSON: `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/healthcare_sap_group_sequential_nsclc/base/input/protocol.json`
- Detailed instructions: `/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/healthcare_sap_group_sequential_nsclc/base/input/task_instructions.md`

## Goal
Create the full SAP deliverable set in:
`/media/user/data/agenthle/health_medicine/healthcare_sap_group_sequential_nsclc/base/output`

Required output filenames:
- `SAP.md`
- `analysis.R`
- `sample_size.json`
- `boundaries.csv`
- `multiple_testing.json`
- `power_curve.csv`
- `power_curve.png`
- `boundary_plot.png`

Read the protocol and instructions, compute the group-sequential design quantities, document the SAP, and include a reproducible R script. Do not use internet access. Hidden reference values and grading logic are not visible during solve time.
agentMustDo: Compute the sample size, interim monitoring boundaries, futility rule, multiplicity allocation, and power curve.
Write the required JSON, CSV, PNG, Markdown, and R script outputs under `output/`.
Use only the staged protocol and instructions; do not use internet access.

inputFiles.name: Protocol
inputFiles.description: Sanitized self-contained NSCLC trial protocol parameters.

referenceFiles.name: Analysis Script
referenceFiles.description: Reproducible R script for generating the outputs.

referenceFiles.name: Numerical Outputs
referenceFiles.description: Sample size, boundaries, multiplicity, power curve, and plot artifacts.

evaluation: The evaluator requires the eight named output files to exist and be non-empty. Sample size must be within 3% of hidden reference values, interim Z boundaries within 0.05, Hochberg alpha allocation to 4 decimals, power-curve CSV structure and monotonicity, non-empty PNG plots, and sufficiently complete SAP/script documentation. Harmless extra top-level files are ignored.

classifier.rationale: The task involves creating a statistical analysis plan for a clinical trial.
